entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2605 - Available Now
entry.content:
Spring has arrived and so has SAP IBP’s 2605 release! Discover our flourishing collection of enhancements designed to transform your planning experience:

This release spotlights the powerful Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) feature that transforms supplier operations from manual monitoring to intelligent automation, helping you maintain optimal stock levels while your planning teams focus on what matters most.

Harmonized scenario management delivers controlled shine-through functionality that extends to orders, allowing you to precisely manage how baseline changes flow through your planning environment.  This capability, previously exclusive to Planner Workspaces, is now also available in the SAP IBP add-in for Microsoft Excel.

SAP IBP 2605 also brings sophisticated new product forecasting that taps into promotion data and other variables for more accurate predictions. Plus, you’ll discover refreshed table designs, powerful formula controls with live feedback, one-click forecast simulations in Planner Workspaces, and Joule’s new AI-powered security compliance checks:

Supply

Smarter Replenishment, Less Manual Work with Vendor Managed Inventory

SAP IBP 2605 introduces Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) with automated replenishment planning, enabling suppliers to plan, trigger and manage customer stock replenishment with greater precision. This enhancement addresses a critical challenge in supplier operations where delayed replenishment decisions put service levels at risk.

The new capabilities shift VMI from reactive, manual monitoring to a proactive, system-driven model that keeps customer locations stocked while freeing planners for higher-value work. As supplier networks continue to grow, manual replenishment approaches simply don’t scale effectively. By embedding replenishment intelligence directly into the planning engine, SAP IBP 2605 closes the gap between inventory signals and execution.

The solution integrates customer demand and inventory visibility directly into SAP IBP supply planning runs, extending the downstream network capabilities within SAP IBP.  Automated replenishment orders flow seamlessly into ERP systems via real-time integration, ensuring smooth execution without manual handoffs. Exception-based alerts notify planners of deviations that require attention, but these alerts don’t disrupt the automated flows, maintaining operational efficiency while ensuring appropriate oversight.

 

Demand

Sophisticated Forecasting Intelligence for New Products

New product forecasting in SAP IBP 2605 gains sophisticated modeling power through an extended like-modeling logic that handles independent variables, including promotion data.

This enhancement delivers improved forecast accuracy through more precise predictions for new product launches. Organizations benefit from streamlined planning by eliminating manual data copying and the need for complex realignment projects that previously consumed valuable time and resources.

The implementation is straightforward - simply switch on like-modelling logic for independent variables via a global configuration parameter. Once activated, new products automatically inherit the promotion and other forecast input data of their predecessors, ensuring highly accurate forecasts from day one.

 

 

Planning UIs

Faster Planning with Refreshed Table Design, Powerful Formula Control, and One-Click Forecast Simulations

Experience a refreshed, contemporary design across Planner Workspaces, with newly redesigned tables in planning views and master data views that deliver a cleaner, more contemporary feel, enabling faster data scanning and intuitive navigation.

Formula editing provides live feedback as you type for improved productivity. Take control of total-level calculations by choosing between aggregating detailed calculations or performing individual formula calculations at the total level, perfect for percentage-based metrics. You can create more than three formulas in a single planning view, and calculate across planning levels - for example, aggregating values from “Product-Location/Ship-From Location” up to “Product-Location” and continuing calculations from there.

Statistical forecasting can now run directly in simulation mode, so you can explore outcomes without committing upfront. Not happy with the results? Discard all changes in a single click, no scenario setup required. And when you want to repeat the last forecast, a one-click re-run action makes iterative forecasting faster and more user-friendly than ever.

 

Foundation

AI-Assisted System Security Checks for SAP IBP

Keeping your SAP IBP system secure just got easier. Joule now compares your actual system configuration against SAP's security recommendations. By instantly surfacing your compliance status and pinpointing exactly where adjustments are needed it reduces security vulnerabilities and offers real-time compliance monitoring and precise remediation.

No more manual audits or guesswork. Simply ask Joule about a specific security topic or recommendation, and it returns a clear compliance assessment along with actionable guidance to close any gaps.

You can request recommendations for a specific topic, check compliance for an individual recommendation item, or run a full compliance check across all security recommendations at once.

Security compliance checks that once required significant time and expertise can now be completed in seconds, giving teams confidence that their system is configured to SAP's security standards.

 

Help us make SAP IBP work better for you! 

Your feedback helps us understand what’s working and what needs attention. Fill in the Product Experience Survey and tell us what you think. 

 

  • SAP IBP What’s New Webinars 2605
  • 2605 What’s New: Help Portal Documentation
  • What’s New in Best Practices
  • SAP IBP Community Page
  • SAP IBP Roadmap
  • SAP Customer Influence Portal for SAP IBP
  • Questions? Ask here
  • SAP IBP Learning Journey

entry.title: Something Exciting is on the Horizon: Your Sapphire 2026 Session Guide
entry.content:
If you have not already, check out Mark Wright's blog post on What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development — it sets the stage perfectly for what is coming.

Sapphire 2026  is shaping up to be one of the most exciting yet. This year, we will be unveiling a wave of new AI features that our team has been developing behind the scenes. We cannot wait to share them with you.

Whether you are just getting started with SAP Build and Joule Studio or you are already an expert, there is something here for you. Our sessions are designed to meet you where you are — giving you the insights, demos, and hands-on learning to take your skills to the next level.

Join us at Sapphire 2026 — register for the format that works best for you:

  • SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference Orlando
  • SAP Sapphire Virtual
  • SAP Sapphire Madrid

 

If you are planning to visit SAP Sapphire in person, stop by our live event demo booth to meet the team and see innovation in action. Discover how SAP Build helps teams create AI-powered solutions—from extensions to workflows and agentic apps—so you can deliver faster with more agility and less complexity. Do not miss our Agent Lab experience, where you can take part in a hands-on session and see just how easy it is to build AI agents that streamline and simplify your daily work:

  • Orlando station (BTP2301) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP2788)
  • Madrid station (BTP1408) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP1537)

 

To help you plan your agenda, we have also curated a selection of must-attend sessions. These highlight the latest AI capabilities for agent-based applications and workflow development, bringing them to life through real-world examples.

One session, however, stands out as essential: “The Enterprise Path to Agentic AI” (BTP1454). Presented by Michael Gall, Sid Misra, and Corey Swenson, this session explores how Joule Studio enables the creation of AI solutions ranging from simple extensions to fully agentic applications. It also highlights SAP’s strategy for delivering an enterprise-grade, intent-to-execution platform tailored for SAP customers. Attendees will see how the Joule solution provides a unified, guided experience — leading developers from initial intent through blueprinting and agent specification to testing and deployment.
